 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-4mk","title":"fcp.reddit: Subreddit Browsing and Search","description":"# fcp.reddit: Subreddit Browsing and Search (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable discovery of relevant communities and threads:
- browse subreddits
- search for subreddits
- search posts across subreddit/all

## Scope
- Get subreddit metadata (rules, description, NSFW/quarantine flags).
- List posts by sort (hot/new/top).
- Search posts with query + filters.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.read`
- `reddit.search`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to Reddit API hosts.
- Content is untrusted/tainted by default.
- Respect content restrictions:
  - NSFW/quarantined handling must be policy-controlled

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.subreddit.get`
  - `reddit.subreddit.search`
  - `reddit.subreddit.posts.list`
  - `reddit.search.posts`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - rate limit headers handling
  - NSFW/quarantine gating

## Acceptance criteria
- Browsing/search is reliable and policy-respecting.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for streaming and SSE library.

## Test Categories

### Server-Sent Events (SSE)
- [ ] Event parsing (data, event, id, retry fields)
- [ ] Multi-line data handling
- [ ] Event ID tracking for reconnection
- [ ] Retry interval handling
- [ ] Connection keep-alive

### WebSocket Support
- [ ] Connection establishment
- [ ] Message framing (text, binary)
- [ ] Ping/pong handling
- [ ] Graceful close
- [ ] Reconnection with backoff

### Stream Processing
- [ ] Backpressure handling
- [ ] Buffer management
- [ ] Stream cancellation
- [ ] Timeout enforcement

### Error Recovery
- [ ] Network disconnection
- [ ] Partial message handling
- [ ] Reconnection with last-event-id
- [ ] Maximum reconnection attempts

### Performance
- [ ] Zero-copy parsing where possible
- [ ] Memory allocation limits
- [ ] Large message handling

## Mocking Strategy
- Mock SSE endpoints returning controlled event streams
- Mock WebSocket servers with programmable responses
- Inject network errors at specific points

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-4px2f","title":"[REVIEW][MEDIUM] fcp-webhook/handler.rs: expired replay-cache entries can pin ReplayCacheFull for up to 60s","description":"file: crates/fcp-webhook/src/handler.rs:297-325

repro:
1. Build a WebhookHandler with idempotency enabled and idempotency_ttl set to a short duration such as 1ms.
2. Fill the replay cache to MAX_SEEN_EVENTS with unique IDs.
3. Wait for the TTL to expire, but for less than 60 seconds.
4. Call claim_event() with a fresh event_id.
5. claim_event returns ReplayCacheFull because cleanup_seen_events() skips pruning until CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MILLIS elapses, even though the cache entries are already expired.

suggested fix:
On the cache-full path, force a stale-entry prune before returning ReplayCacheFull, or tie the cleanup cadence to min(idempotency_ttl, CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MILLIS) so expired entries cannot block new deliveries for up to a minute.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:04:07.593351Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T01:26:26.514804Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T01:26:26.514259Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2899,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-4px2f","author":"jemanuel","text":"Verified work landed in commit 0b788d26 (2026-04-25): added prune_expired_events_locked() helper called on the cache-full path in both claim_event() and record_event() before returning ReplayCacheFull, plus regression test test_claim_event_prunes_expired_entries_when_cache_is_full exercising MAX_SEEN_EVENTS expired entries with skipped periodic cleanup. All 86 fcp-webhook handler tests pass. Closing bead.","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:26:26Z"}]}

Repro: mint a capability token for principal user:alice, send /rpc/preflight or /rpc/simulate with body principal user:alice and HTTP header X-Principal: user:bob; those handlers ignore headers and evaluate against request.principal, while /rpc/invoke and /rpc/batch deny on the same header mismatch.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-9fxph","title":"Make hard-coded timeout, buffer, and retry constants configurable across infrastructure crates","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:08.375964Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:25:58.951243Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:25:58.951069Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config","hardening","infra"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9fxph","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1z643","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:29.209947Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1951,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9fxph","author":"jemanuel","text":"EPIC: Configurable Timeout/Buffer/Retry Infrastructure

The infrastructure crates (fcp-streaming, fcp-webhook, fcp-sdk, fcp-ratelimit, fcp-telemetry, fcp-store, fcp-sandbox) define timeout, buffer, retry, and limit constants as hard-coded values in Default impls or module-level consts. While many have builder patterns for programmatic override, none support environment-variable or config-file override.

This matters because:
1. Operators need to tune timeouts for their network conditions without recompilation
2. Different connectors have wildly different latency profiles (a webhook timeout of 5min is fine for Stripe but wrong for a real-time chat)
3. The FCP zone model should allow per-zone tuning (z:owner on a fast LAN vs z:community over DERP relay)
4. CI environments often need shorter timeouts than production

CURRENT STATE (hard-coded values found):
- fcp-streaming: 1s reconnect delay, 60s max delay, 30s heartbeat timeout, 120s zombie timeout, 10 max reconnects, 8KB buffer, 1MB SSE buffer, 64MB max WS message
- fcp-webhook: 300s timestamp tolerance, 5MB max payload
- fcp-sdk: 30s rate-limit retry-after, 256KB max JSON, 1000 max array len, 32 max depth, 5 max schema errors
- fcp-ratelimit: 1s/60s base/max backoff, 100k sliding window cap, 60/1000 per-minute presets
- fcp-telemetry: port 9090 for Prometheus
- fcp-store: 256MB max object store size
- fcp-sandbox: 100k WASM table elements

All of these have reasonable defaults. The issue is not the values but the inability to override them without code changes.

FIX APPROACH:
1. Create a shared ConfigValue<T> pattern that tries: explicit param > env var > compiled default
2. Apply this pattern to the most impactful constants first (timeouts, buffer sizes)
3. Document env var names in each crate's module docs
4. Keep compiled defaults unchanged (no behavior change for existing users)

This epic does NOT cover per-connector timeout overrides (those are a separate concern — connectors already have ConnectorRuntimeConfig with builder pattern).","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:29Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-9hh","title":"fcp.arxiv: CASS integration for paper indexing and retrieval","description":"# fcp.arxiv: CASS integration for paper indexing and retrieval (FCP2)

## Goal
Integrate arXiv content with CASS (Conversation Archive & Semantic Search) so agents can:
- index papers as searchable chunks with provenance
- retrieve indexed content efficiently for later queries

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Scope
- Convert paper content (abstract + sections) into chunk objects:
  - stable chunking rules
  - stable chunk IDs
- Attach provenance metadata:
  - arXiv id/version
  - extraction method
  - timestamps
- Write chunks into CASS (policy-controlled capability).
- Query CASS for indexed papers (semantic or keyword, depending on CASS capabilities).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `arxiv.paper.read`
- `arxiv.pdf.extract`
- `cass.write` (dangerous-ish; persistence)
- `cass.search` (read)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- No hidden network egress beyond pinned arXiv hosts.
- CASS writes must be capability gated and auditable.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `arxiv.cass.index_paper`
  - `arxiv.cass.get_index_status`
  - Optional: `arxiv.cass.search`
- Canonical types:
  - `CassIndexStatus`
  - `CassChunkDescriptor`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Fixture-based tests for deterministic chunking.
- Mock-only tests for:
  - CASS write/search calls (no real external calls in CI)
  - audit/receipt emission on writes

## Acceptance criteria
- Indexing is deterministic, bounded, and provenance-rich.
- CASS writes are mechanically authorized and auditable.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-9hoz","title":"TEST-MESH: MeshNode real-component integration (no mocks)","description":"# TEST-MESH: MeshNode real-component integration (no mocks)

## Goal
Use the real MeshNode stack (sessions, gossip, admission control, leases) in deterministic multi-node tests without mocks.

## Scope
- fcp-mesh session handshake, replay policy, routing.
- admission control and anti-amplification enforcement.
- gossip convergence and checkpoint exchange.

## Constraints
- Use actual networking primitives in-process or loopback; no mocked MeshNode or fake transports.
- Deterministic seed and controlled timing for repeatability.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Multi-node integration suite validates allow and deny paths with DecisionReceipt evidence.
- Admission control rejects flood scenarios with correct reason codes.
- Logs are structured JSON for each phase.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-9jxao","title":"[REVIEW-A9][HIGH] crates/fcp-raptorq/src/decode.rs:385: dense fallback allocates outside hostile-decode budget","description":"RaptorQDecoder::validate_decode_bounds() only budgets buffered symbol bytes (decode.rs:625-633), but try_reconstruct() can force dense_fallback() on a bogus payload_hash or any recoverable decode miss (decode.rs:300-321). try_reconstruct_dense() then allocates ConstraintMatrix::zeros(total_rows, l) plus ConstraintMatrix::build(params, 0) and RHS buffers (decode.rs:384-389), which is O(L^2) bytes and can exceed GiB for legal K near the RFC max. Repro: with default config and a max-supported OTI (~56k source symbols, 1024-byte symbols) carrying an incorrect payload_hash, feed valid source symbols until decode succeeds; the hash mismatch forces dense fallback and a multi-GiB matrix allocation even though ingress budgeting allowed only ~tens of MiB.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j5f1d","title":"[REVIEW-A9][MEDIUM] fcp-sandbox/wasi.rs:1261: missing writable preopens widen file grants into directory authority","description":"crates/fcp-sandbox/src/wasi.rs:1261-1290 auto-creates any missing writable preopen path with create_dir_all(path) and then mounts it as a directory. Existing file paths are rejected by validate_preopened_paths at 1341-1353, but missing file-like paths are not distinguished from intended directories.

Concrete repro:
1. Build a WasiConfig whose writable_paths contains a missing path such as `/tmp/fcp-secret.json`.
2. Call WasiRuntime::new(config) and then create_store().
3. prepare_preopened_dir() creates `/tmp/fcp-secret.json/` as a directory and preopens it with DirPerms::all() / FilePerms::all().
4. The guest now has directory authority under that path (`/tmp/fcp-secret.json/anything`) even though a manifest author could reasonably have intended a single writable file path.

This is a capability widening bug, not just a UX quirk: file-scoped allowlists are rejected only when the file already exists, so a missing leaf bypasses that guard and is materialized as a writable directory.

Companion validation gap: crates/fcp-manifest/src/lib.rs sandbox path validation accepts absolute file-like strings without any directory/file distinction, so the WASI layer receives ambiguous writable path declarations.

Fix direction: fail closed on missing writable preopen paths unless the manifest/schema can distinguish directory preopens from file grants, or add an explicit directory-only marker and reject ambiguous file-like writable entries before create_dir_all.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T23:26:05.125551Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:30:03.099708Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T23:30:03.099479Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of jgtlf","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j6ps","title":"[IDEA-22] Device Posture Attestation Enforcement","description":"## Background
The README and RFCs describe optional hardware-backed device posture attestations for high-trust zones, but enforcement is not yet mechanical.

## Goals
- Introduce `DevicePostureAttestation` objects and verification rules.
- Allow zone policies to require hardware-backed keys (TPM/Secure Enclave).
- Fail closed when posture is missing or invalid.

## Non-goals
- Implementing hardware attestation collection itself for every OS in this iteration (focus on verification and policy).

## Approach
- Define canonical schema for posture attestation.
- Extend enrollment validation and zone policy evaluation to require posture.
- Add test fixtures for valid/invalid posture.

## Risks
- Platform variance in attestation formats; keep schema extensible and versioned.

## Success Criteria
- Zones can enforce posture requirements with stable reason codes.
- Tests cover accept/deny paths.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Zones can enforce posture requirement with stable deny reason.
- Enrollment without posture fails closed in required zones.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-s7j5","title":"TEST: Anthropic Connector Integration Tests","description":"# TEST: Anthropic Connector Integration Tests (Deterministic)


## Goal
Validate Anthropic connector integration flows under deterministic mocks (streaming, tool use) and compliance checks.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Purpose
Integration tests for `fcp.anthropic` using a deterministic mock Anthropic API (wiremock + SSE simulation).

No real Anthropic calls are allowed.

## Must cover
- Non-streaming generation.
- Streaming SSE generation:
  - chunk parsing
  - cancellation
  - error mid-stream
- Tool/function calling request/response shapes.
- Error taxonomy mapping (401/429/5xx).
- Usage metrics extraction (tokens, latencies) — **not hard-coded currency pricing**.
- FCP2 contract: default deny / allow + DecisionReceipt reason codes.

## Logging
- Structured JSON logs with correlation IDs.
- No API key leakage; no raw prompts/responses by default.

## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-s9v","title":"fcp.docusign: Download Signed Documents","description":"# fcp.docusign: Download Signed Documents (FCP2)

## Goal
Allow retrieval of completed, signed documents safely:
- list documents in an envelope
- download document bytes (streamed, bounded)

Signed documents may contain highly sensitive data.

## Scope
- List envelope documents (metadata).
- Download a document.
- Optional: download combined document bundle.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `docusign.documents.read` (dangerous; sensitive content)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Strict IO bounds:
  - max bytes
  - streaming download
  - hash computation for evidence
- Never log document contents.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `docusign.document.list`
  - `docusign.document.download`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - download bounds
  - host pinning
  - hash correctness

## Acceptance criteria
- Document downloads are safe-by-default and do not allow unbounded memory usage.

- Root cause: `StreamHealthTracker::record_disconnect()` treated `max_reconnect_attempts` as an exclusive upper bound (`>`), which contradicted both the docs and the shared `ReconnectHandler` contract (`attempts < max`).
- Fix: switch the boundary to `>=` so the tracker becomes `Unhealthy` exactly when the reconnect budget is exhausted, not one disconnect later.
- Regression coverage: updated `max_reconnect_attempts_makes_unhealthy`, updated `simulated_max_reconnects_exhaustion`, and added `zero_max_reconnect_attempts_fails_first_disconnect`.

- Root cause: the advertised history env overrides were broken in two ways: malformed env var names (`FWC_max_log_size()_BYTES`, `FWC_max_rotated_files()`) that could not realistically be set, and `HistoryFilter::new()` ignored the documented `FWC_HISTORY_DEFAULT_LIMIT` override entirely.

I attempted to patch this with a serialized early-return guard, but writes to that file failed under filesystem pressure (`ENOSPC`).

3. connectors/gcp: endpoint overrides validated only host/scheme even though the client treats them as plain origins before appending service paths. Fixed by rejecting path/query/fragment on non-local Google API overrides and adding regression coverage for pathful overrides.

4. connectors/docusign: integration tests exposed a contract mismatch where docusign.stream_connect_events was advertised in introspection/tests but still returned an unimplemented error. Fixed by returning a deterministic empty poll result with streaming=true and optional since_ts echo until real Connect polling lands.

This touches fcp-host (lifecycle management) and fwc (lifecycle commands). COMPLEXITY NOTE: Type-state patterns are elegant but can be harder to work with in dynamic dispatch scenarios. If fcp-host stores LifecycleRecords in a heterogeneous collection, a Box<dyn LifecycleState> erased type may be needed. Consider a hybrid: type-state for compile-time transition validation, with an erased AnyLifecycleRecord for storage. ACCEPTANCE: (1) Each lifecycle state is a distinct type. (2) Transition methods only on valid source states. (3) Invalid transitions are compile errors. (4) Erased storage type for dynamic dispatch.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-wwq8","title":"[FCP2] DeviceProfile: Capability Reporting, Resource Constraints, Execution Fitness","description":"# [FCP2] DeviceProfile: Capability Reporting, Resource Constraints, Execution Fitness

## Goal
Define and implement the DeviceProfile schema used by the execution planner to make device-aware routing and execution decisions.

## Why This Matters
The spec envisions operations routing to devices based on capabilities (GPU, TPU, memory, battery, network). Without a concrete DeviceProfile schema, execution planning is guesswork.

## DeviceProfile Schema (NORMATIVE)

### Hardware Capabilities
```
DeviceProfile {
  node_id: TailscaleNodeId,
  profile_version: u32,
  timestamp: u64,
  
  // Compute
  cpu_cores: u16,
  cpu_arch: CpuArch,  // x86_64, aarch64, wasm32
  memory_mb: u32,
  gpu: Option<GpuProfile>,
  tpu: Option<TpuProfile>,
  
  // Storage
  local_storage_mb: u64,
  symbol_store_quota_mb: u32,
  
  // Power
  power_source: PowerSource,  // Mains, Battery(percent), Solar
  battery_percent: Option<u8>,
  
  // Network
  bandwidth_estimate_kbps: u32,
  latency_class: LatencyClass,  // Local, LAN, Internet, DERP
  metered: bool,
  
  // Availability
  availability: AvailabilityProfile,  // AlwaysOn, Scheduled, BestEffort
  next_expected_downtime: Option<u64>,
  
  // Installed Connectors
  connectors: Vec<InstalledConnector>,
}
```

### GpuProfile
```
GpuProfile {
  vendor: GpuVendor,  // Nvidia, AMD, Apple, Intel
  model: String,
  vram_mb: u32,
  compute_capability: Option<String>,  // CUDA version, etc.
}
```

### InstalledConnector
```
InstalledConnector {
  connector_id: ConnectorId,
  version: Version,
  binary_hash: ObjectId,
  capabilities: Vec<CapabilityGrant>,
}
```

## Profile Publication
- Nodes MUST publish DeviceProfile to mesh on:
  - startup
  - significant capability change
  - connector install/remove
  - periodic refresh (every 5min default)
- Profile is a canonical mesh object (content-addressed)
- Profiles are gossipped to all zone members

## Execution Fitness Scoring
The execution planner uses DeviceProfile to compute fitness:
```
fitness = base_score
  - derp_penalty (if would use DERP)
  + locality_bonus (if symbols already present)
  - battery_penalty (if on battery < 20%)
  + capability_bonus (if has required GPU/TPU)
  - latency_penalty (based on latency_class)
```

## Tests
- Unit tests: DeviceProfile serialization roundtrip
- Unit tests: fitness scoring with various profiles
- Integration tests: profile gossip propagation
- Property tests: fitness ordering is stable and deterministic

## Acceptance Criteria
- DeviceProfile schema is canonical and versioned
- Execution planner can query available profiles and compute fitness
- Profile changes trigger re-evaluation of pending operations

## Goal
Validate Tailscale integration (identity, attestations, peer discovery, ACL mapping) with deterministic tests/mocks.

## Scope
Comprehensive tests for Tailscale mesh identity and peer discovery.

## Test Categories

### MeshIdentity
- [ ] Node ID derived from WireGuard pubkey
- [ ] Identity is stable across restarts
- [ ] Identity is unique per device
- [ ] Identity format is canonical

### NodeKeyAttestation
- [ ] Attestation binds node signing key to mesh identity
- [ ] Attestation is owner-signed
- [ ] Attestation includes timestamp
- [ ] Stale attestation detection
- [ ] Attestation verification

### Peer Discovery
- [ ] Peers discovered via Tailscale API
- [ ] Peer list updates on join/leave
- [ ] Peer health monitoring
- [ ] Peer capability detection
- [ ] Peer filtering by ACL tags

### ACL Tag Mapping
- [ ] tag:fcp-owner maps to z:owner
- [ ] tag:fcp-private maps to z:private
- [ ] tag:fcp-work maps to z:work
- [ ] tag:fcp-community maps to z:community
- [ ] tag:fcp-public maps to z:public
- [ ] Unknown tags handled gracefully

### Transport Security
- [ ] FCPS uses WireGuard encryption
- [ ] Additional AEAD layer verified
- [ ] No plaintext fallback
- [ ] Connection failures logged

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/tailscale/`
- `mesh_identity.cbor`
- `node_key_attestation.cbor`
- `peer_discovery_response.json`

## Logging Requirements
```json

  "phase": "execute",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "node_id": "...",
  "peers_discovered": 3,
  "acl_tags": ["tag:fcp-work"],
  "result": "pass"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Mesh identity is stable and unique
- Peer discovery works reliably
- ACL tags map correctly to zones
